name: Invoice
description: A billing document requesting payment for goods or services rendered. Tracks amounts, dates, payment status, and line-item details across the invoice lifecycle from draft to paid.

semantics.definition: A unique sequential or formatted identifier for the invoice, used for reference in accounting systems, communications, and payment reconciliation.
The total monetary value of the invoice including all line items, taxes, and adjustments.
The current state of the invoice in its lifecycle, from creation through to payment or cancellation.
The date the invoice was formally issued to the customer, starting the payment terms clock.
The date by which payment is expected, calculated from issue date plus payment terms.
The date on which the invoice was fully paid, used for cash flow reporting and reconciliation.
A description of the goods or services billed on this invoice, capturing what the customer is being charged for.
The method used or expected for payment of this invoice.
Additional notes or context about the invoice, such as special terms, discounts applied, or internal references.

An invoice is a formal billing document that records amounts owed for goods or services. It serves as the primary financial artifact linking customer relationships to revenue recognition and cash flow tracking.
